description: External pressure coefficients (Cp) for MWFRS — Directional Procedure
note: All Cp values are used in: p = q * G * Cp - qi * GCpi

windward_wall.note: Constant regardless of L/B ratio. Use qz (varies with height) for windward wall.

side_walls.note: Use qh (constant at mean roof height)

leeward_wall.description: Varies with L/B ratio — L is dimension parallel to wind
leeward_wall.note: Use qh (constant at mean roof height). Interpolate between L/B values.
leeward_wall.interpolation: Linear interpolation permitted between tabulated L/B values

roofs.note: Roof Cp depends on roof type, slope, h/L ratio, and wind direction. Multiple zones apply simultaneously.

zones_by_h_over_L.description: Zones are measured as horizontal distance from windward edge

zone_0_to_h.note: Two values — use both, worst case governs
Two values — use both, worst case governs
Two values — use both, worst case governs

gable_roof.description: Two slopes meeting at ridge — evaluate wind normal and parallel to ridge

wind_normal_to_ridge.description: Wind perpendicular to the ridge line

windward_slope.note: Cp varies with slope angle and h/L ratio

slope_30to45deg.note: Positive Cp — pressure toward surface (uplift becomes downward force)

slope_60deg.note: Use 0.01 * slope angle in degrees for slopes between 45 and 60

leeward_slope.note: Cp is constant regardless of h/L ratio for leeward slope

wind_parallel_to_ridge.description: Wind blowing along the length of the ridge
wind_parallel_to_ridge.note: Both slopes treated as leeward — use same Cp zones as flat roof based on h/L

zone_0_to_h_over_2.note: Suction in all cases for parallel wind

monoslope_roof.note: Cp values vary significantly with slope, h/L ratio, and wind direction. Refer to Fig. 27.3-3 directly — not tabulated here due to complexity.

hip_roof.note: Hip roofs are treated similarly to gable roofs for MWFRS per ASCE 7-16. Use gable roof Cp values as conservative approximation, or refer to project-specific analysis.
